change_details: ReplayRatioController unit-mismatch fix: convert per-game spDelay to per-tick overhead before subtracting from barrier-tick wall. Previous fix subtracted full per-game value (~500ms) from per-tick wall (~30ms), driving controller into the opposite overshoot (cur=2.48 vs target 1.10). Multiplying by positionsProduced/G gives correct per-tick estimate.
rationale: In recordSelfPlayBarrierTick, currentDelaySettingMs is per-game (the BatchedSelfPlayDriver sleeps it once per worker per game-end). The barrier tick is per-ply across the pool. Subtracting per-game ms directly from per-tick wall is a unit error. With G = _lastSelfPlayPositionsPerGame and positionsProduced ply-submissions per tick, expected game-ends across the pool per tick = positionsProduced/G. Each game-end inflates the barrier wall by ~currentDelaySettingMs (the barrier waits for the slowest worker). So the correct per-tick overhead estimate is currentDelaySettingMs × positionsProduced / G. Numbers from the failing run: spDelay≈500ms, N=48, G≈370, predicted overhead ≈65ms, actual barrier wall ≈30ms — matches: the controller was subtracting 16x too much, getting wildly negative effective elapsed and pumping the smoothed sp delay way up in response.
